subject: general
title: How to check if all 45 students understood today's lesson
query_text: How to check if all 45 students understood today's lesson

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Ask all students to show a thumbs up (understood), thumbs sideways (a little confused), or thumbs down (did not understand) — scan the room quickly
Pick 3–4 students from different ability groups and ask them to explain one key idea from the lesson in their own words
Write one simple question on the board related to today's lesson and ask every student to write the answer on their slate or a scrap of paper — collect or scan responses in 2 minutes

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — Exit Slip on Slate
classroom_activity.activity: In the last 5 minutes of class, write one question on the board (e.g., for Math: '35 Tens = ___ ?' or 'What is 4596 + 104?'). Every student writes the answer on their slate or notebook. Ask students to hold up slates simultaneously. You can see at a glance who got it right, who is unsure, and who needs help. Group students mentally into three: confident, partially understood, need support.
classroom_activity.materials: Blackboard, chalk, student slates or notebooks

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: For students who show confusion, pair them with a peer who understood well. Ask the peer to explain using an example — this reinforces learning for both. This peer-teaching approach works well in mixed-ability classrooms of 40+ students without requiring extra materials.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After the slate activity, which group was largest — confident, partial, or confused — and what does that tell you about tomorrow's starting point?

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: TaRL Methodology (Pratham) — grouping by understanding level; NCERT Class-4 Mathematics Teacher Notes — encouraging students to share reasoning; NEP 2020 Section 4.6 — formative assessment as a regular classroom practice
